mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) captures real output momentum. Positive momentum signals ongoing economic activity that supports corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank correlation between INDPRO change and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns do not exceed 50% baseline in the top quintile of INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% represents restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and discount rates, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered months, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in consumer spending and rising unemployment create a demand collapse signal. The interaction of falling retail sales (RSAFS) and increasing unemployment (UNRATE) forecasts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because earnings expectations are revised down across broad sectors.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month while UNRATE increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The combined signal of RSAFS decline with UNRATE increase fails to produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of joint occurrences, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a slowdown in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates that restrictive policy is curbing labor demand. This regime-specific weakness translates into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate falling consumption and Fed overtightening.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, PAYEMS contractions do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of residential investment and downstream consumer durable demand. A negative trend in HOUST predicts weaker economic activity and therefore negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative correlation between monthly HOUST changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not statistically significant at the 5% level over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpectedly large monthly increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) raises inflation risk premiums and expected policy rates, leading to rapid repricing of equities and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Following CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes above 0.3%, positive 5-day SPX returns occur in more than 55% of instances, or the average return is not negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) revises the trajectory of aggregate demand and corporate profits. Positive GDPC1 surprises lead to upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns do not dominate in the top GDP-growth quintile.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating unemployment rate (UNRATE increase over 2 consecutive months) predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as labor income risk rises and consumption expectations fall.
failure_condition: Two consecutive monthly increases in UNRATE do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.
